_comment: DISPATCHER seccomp profile for the packet-analysis CONTAINER (ADR-0049 blocker 5). This is the profile the packet-analysis container runs under at the CONTAINER level — NOT the strict child profile. The executor-split (ADR-0049) draws the sandbox boundary around the untrusted pcap parse, not the Celery machinery: the long-lived DISPATCHER process is a broker-connected Celery consumer that needs client sockets (Redis/PG/DNS) and the seccomp()/prctl() syscalls to spawn a SELF-CONFINING child (python -m app.engines.packet.executor). That child re-applies the STRICT deny-by-default profile (packet-analysis-seccomp.json) with prctl(PR_SET_NO_NEW_PRIVS)+seccomp() BEFORE it opens a single pcap byte, so tshark's C dissectors run with NO socket at all (the strict filter denies socket()). This dispatcher profile is therefore the STRICT profile PLUS the minimum a broker-connected worker + a self-confining parent need: the client-socket family, seccomp(), and (already in the strict base) prctl(). It STILL denies every escape/raw primitive the strict profile denies — socket(AF_PACKET)/socket(AF_NETLINK) (arg-filtered below), bpf, ptrace, mount, unshare, setns, keyctl/add_key/request_key, process_vm_readv/writev, kexec, init_module, personality — by the SCMP_ACT_ERRNO default action. NET_RAW is absent (cap_drop:[ALL], no add), so even the allowed socket() cannot open an AF_PACKET SOCK_RAW capture socket. Byte-identical to deploy/kubernetes/netops/seccomp/packet-analysis-dispatcher-seccomp.json (the chart's ConfigMap/localhostProfile copy) and pinned by the extended ADR-0049 blocker-3 lockstep CI gate.

syscalls.comment: file I/O and directory ops: read the pcap, write to the bounded /tmp scratch (ADR-0031 §2). No socket/network syscalls here.
memory management for the C dissectors and the Python heap. mprotect is allowed for the runtime; W^X is enforced by readOnlyRootFilesystem + no-new-privs, not by denying mprotect.

process/thread lifecycle so the Python parent can spawn the tshark child (argv, not shell — ADR-0023 §1) and reap it. clone/clone3 flags that create new namespaces are still blocked because unshare/setns are denied by the default action. setsid is DISPATCHER-only: it is the session-leader syscall CPython issues in the forked child when the dispatcher spawns the executor with start_new_session=True (ADR-0049 blocker 4 — the child must lead its own session/process group so a wedged/popped tshark grandchild dies with the group on killpg). Denying it EPERMs every Popen and kills the enforced analysis tier outright (review F1). It is NOT a sandbox-escape primitive (it only detaches the caller into a new session — no namespace, mount, or privilege change) and stays OUT of the strict child profile: the executor never calls setsid.

benign process introspection + time + RNG that glibc/Python need at startup. prctl is allowed for PR_SET_NAME/PR_SET_NO_NEW_PRIVS; capset is NOT allowed (the parser drops to zero caps and never re-acquires). Note the absence of bpf(), ptrace(), mount(), unshare(), setns(), keyctl(), add_key(), request_key(), process_vm_readv/writev(), kexec_load(), init_module(), finit_module(), and personality() — each is a known sandbox-escape or raw-socket primitive and is denied by SCMP_ACT_ERRNO. socket() is arg-filtered below (client families only), NOT allowed unconditionally.

runc >=1.2 container init (new mount API). fsopen/fsconfig/fsmount/fspick/open_tree/move_mount/mount_setattr are required by runc's safe re-open of /proc/self/fd — the CVE-2024-21626 ("Leaky Vessels") mitigation — and statfs/fstatfs verify the reopened handle is really procfs (PROC_SUPER_MAGIC). Without these the analysis container cannot start and crash-loops: 'reopen exec fifo: get safe /proc/thread-self/fd handle: fstatfs fsmount:fscontext:proc: operation not permitted'. These mirror moby's own default hardened profile. They are INERT without CAP_SYS_ADMIN, which this sandbox drops (cap_drop:ALL + no-new-privileges): the unprivileged parser still cannot mount a filesystem, pivot_root, or change namespaces — the classic mount()/unshare()/setns() escape primitives remain denied by the default action (SCMP_ACT_ERRNO).

celery/billiard POSIX named-semaphore creation: glibc sem_open(O_CREAT) atomically creates /dev/shm/sem.<name> via a temp-file-then-link() dance, so a denied link() crashes the celery prefork worker at boot ('PermissionError: [Errno 1] Operation not permitted' in _billiard.SemLock). link/linkat create hard links only (no new content, same filesystem, write required on the target dir — reachable only inside the writable tmpfs scratch and /dev/shm here); they are NOT a sandbox-escape primitive and are allowed unconditionally by moby's default profile. The pcap is on a read-only mount and cannot be hard-linked out.

DISPATCHER-only (ADR-0049 blocker 5): the client-socket family a broker-connected Celery consumer needs — connect()/send*/recv*/shutdown for the long-lived Redis broker + Postgres connections, socketpair for the billiard prefork pool's parent<->worker IPC, getsockname/getpeername/get-set-sockopt for connection setup and DNS. This whole group is ABSENT from the strict child profile (packet-analysis-seccomp.json): the executor child re-confines with that stricter filter before touching the pcap, so tshark inherits NO socket capability. socket() itself is arg-filtered (client AF_* only) in the entries below — AF_PACKET/AF_NETLINK are NOT reachable, so no raw-capture or netlink primitive is exposed even here. NET_RAW is absent (cap_drop:[ALL]), so an AF_PACKET SOCK_RAW capture socket cannot be opened regardless.

DISPATCHER-only (ADR-0049 blocker 5): the executor CHILD self-confines by loading the strict libseccomp filter via the seccomp() syscall (SECCOMP_SET_MODE_FILTER). The container profile must therefore permit seccomp() so the child can INSTALL a STRICTER filter on itself (seccomp filters only ever add restrictions — this can never widen the sandbox). The load needs no capability because the child first sets prctl(PR_SET_NO_NEW_PRIVS) (prctl is in the introspection group above). Once the child loads the strict filter (which does NOT list seccomp), a second seccomp() call is denied — the child confines exactly once. seccomp() is absent from the strict child profile.

DISPATCHER-only, ARG-FILTERED (ADR-0049 blocker 5): allow socket() for AF_UNIX/AF_LOCAL (domain==1) only — the billiard prefork pool + local IPC. Any other domain falls through to the SCMP_ACT_ERRNO default. This is the arg-filtered alternative to a blanket socket() allow the blocker prefers.

DISPATCHER-only, ARG-FILTERED: allow socket() for AF_INET (domain==2) — the IPv4 TCP Redis broker + Postgres connections and UDP DNS to the container resolver. AF_PACKET (17) and AF_NETLINK (16) are NOT matched by any entry, so a raw-capture or netlink socket() returns EPERM under the default action. glibc getaddrinfo's optional AF_NETLINK ADDRCONFIG probe fails closed (EPERM) and falls back cleanly, so denying netlink does not break DNS to the container resolver over AF_INET UDP.
DISPATCHER-only, ARG-FILTERED: allow socket() for AF_INET6 (domain==10) — the IPv6 counterpart of the broker/DB/DNS client sockets. Same denial of AF_PACKET/AF_NETLINK as the AF_INET entry.
